reference: Your symptoms are most probably due to a spasm in the paraspinal muscles. These are muscles that lie on either side of your spine. They must have become spasmodic when you were dusting the rugs. I would suggest that you take complete bed rest for a period of one to two days until the pain subsides. However, you can attend to your daily needs and keep it to a minimum of essential activity. You can also find some relief by using hot water compresses over that area for a period of twenty minutes every three hours. Since the pain relievers aren't helping, you should get prescription medications for a period of two weeks, for which you will need to see your physician.

They must have become spasmodic when you were dusting the rugs. I would suggest that you take complete bed rest for a period of one to two days until the pain subsides. However, you can attend to your daily needs and keep it to a minimum of essential activity. You can also find some relief by using hot water compresses over that area for a period of 20 minutes every three hours. Since the pain relievers aren't helping, you should get prescription medications for a period of two weeks, for which you will need to see your physician.
